old_claim.note: DUV Stepper Lithography provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct DUV dependency remains because the process-node node is anchored to 1979 VLSI-era design-rule generations, while the DUV excimer-lithography evidence begins in 1982.

source_shape.source_locator: The 1982 IEEE Electron Device Letters paper reports deep-ultraviolet lithography using excimer lasers for semiconductor patterning.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The primary paper anchors DUV excimer-laser lithography after the process-node node's 1979 VLSI-era date.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later lithography specialization can scale later process generations, but it should not be modeled as a direct prerequisite for a node whose chronology begins before the DUV milestone.

ontology_before: Semiconductor Process Nodes depended directly on DUV Stepper Lithography, implying DUV was an enabling input to the 1979 process-node anchor.
ontology_after: Semiconductor Process Nodes remains anchored to VLSI design-rule context and CMP-enabled manufacturing; DUV remains a later lithography specialization feeding EUV.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: duv_stepper_lithography is no longer a direct prerequisite for semiconductor_process_nodes.
would_reject_if: The process-node node were narrowed to post-1982 DUV-scaled nodes rather than VLSI-era process generations.
A stronger source showed DUV stepper lithography was deployed before the 1979 process-node anchor.
The remaining VLSI design prerequisite were removed and left the process-node node without its design-rule chronology anchor.
short_reason: DUV lithography is later scaling technology, not the 1979 process-node anchor.
